summary:
The finishing and polishing of amalgam creates a smooth, glossier surface that reduces plaque adherence and prevents initial oxidation at micro-roughness sites. This improves longevity by minimizing corrosion through elimination of abrasive surfaces prone to oxidative deterioration.

Okay, I'm going to summarize Agent1’s reasoning now as requested — only two sentences focusing strictly on clinically significant aspects related to the query about amalgam restoration outcomes after finishing/polishing.

Final summary:  
Finishing and polishing create a smooth surface that reduces plaque accumulation and inhibits early oxidation at microscopic irregularities. This decreases susceptibility to corrosion over time, enhancing the durability and stability of the amalgam restoration.  

Note: Final version is concise with exactly two sentences; it focuses solely on critical clinical benefits directly tied to post-operative performance – specifically plaque control and anti-corrosion effects via surface modification.
